channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Untaxed vehicle, 15th July
item.description:
Been here for 2 months now, reported numerous times by local residents and still no visit from DVLA

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Upper grove Road, Upper Belvedere, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: No plate number, 15th July
item.description:
This car is really destroyed. Is not used for something, but stay in car parc for long time. Someone must come to check why doesn’t have plate number.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Swift Close, Thamesmead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Sign clutter, 15th July
item.description:
3 x one way signs and 1x turn left sign. Are all these signs needed?

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 9, South Street, Plaistow, Greater London

Lots of waste bags

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Kimberley Avenue, Nunhead, Greater London

item.title: The car has been parked outside my house for six days, it has not moved, 15th July
item.description:
The car has parked out side my gate for the past six days, No one has attempted to move it

All day

item.title: This tree needs cutting back children’s nursery directly under it, 15th July
item.description:
Saplings childrens nursery is my neighbouring property a public footpath running down the alleyway as a very large tree. This is potentially dangerous for the children in the playground of the nursery. I have also sent an email to the nursery warning them of this danger to the children in the playground.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 89, Beckenham Lane, Shortlands, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Waste left on public pavement, 15th July
item.description:
A used and dirty mattress has been left on the pavement for several days

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Parish Lane, Penge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Graffiti on speed sign, 15th July
item.description:
SV on illuminated speed sign

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): East Road, East Wickham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Graffiti sprayed on two main terracotta pillars by Brixton underground station SW9., 15th July
item.description:
I like to report graffiti sprayed on two terracotta pillars by Brixton underground station . The pillars are situated by the main bicycle racks. There is more graffiti in close proximity to this underneath a railway bridge. Please can they be painted over.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 387, Brixton Road, Brixton, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Building material dumped on pavement, 15th July
item.description:
Some tiles and wooden bits dumped on pavement here

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 90, Belvedere Road, Crystal Palace, Greater London

item.description:
Right outside a school

item.category: Graffiti / Flyposting on stop or shelter (non-offensive)

item.title: Cushions and, 15th July

item.title: Seems like a void forming, 15th July
item.description:
Water has leaked for some time considering the green around it. Ir has created a hole and it gets bigger everyday

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 31, Nichol Lane, Plaistow, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Extremely overgrown grass verges, 15th July
item.description:
This situation has been ongoing for a couple of years now, Prior to this the verges were reasonably maintained. The grass verge here, and in the whole area, is extremely overgrown.. This is not `biodiversity` (which needs proper maintenance and weed control, etc) but has crossed the line into negligence. (My bordering fence is now turning a green colour with moss and mould) and there are real public health concerns. People allow their dogs to foul on this verge and the grass is so high that it prevents those people who actually pick up their dog`s faeces unable to do so as it cannot be seen. Furthermore with a high local fox population, this unmanaged area presents a severe risk of tick infestations next to residential homes. I am unable/unwilling to risk going onto this verge to maintain my own bordering hedge as not safe to do so. Also when you do finally cut this grass which now looks like wheat with huge weeds it will look dreadful as you leave the cuttings there and the grass is dead underneath. Our council tax bills include environmental services. I have no intention of sounding disrespectful but ask simply that you please maintain the grass in the area, especially on considering the consequences of not doing so. Thank you
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 26, Breakspears Drive, St Paul's Cray, Orpington, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
